_comment: Example metrics snapshot for OverSync. All values are illustrative testnet figures. See docs/METRICS_SCHEMA.md for full field definitions.

successful_swaps._description: Total completed swaps (both legs claimed) in the measurement window.
successful_swaps.source: coordinator orders WHERE status='completed', cross-checked against on-chain Claimed events

successful_swap_volume_usd._description: Approximate USD equivalent of completed swap amounts. Testnet tokens have no real economic value; this field validates the derivation pipeline only.
successful_swap_volume_usd.source: HTLCEscrow.OrderCreated.amount × CoinGecko price at block timestamp

refund_count._description: Number of orders that ended in a refund (either side) in the measurement window.
refund_count.source: coordinator orders WHERE status='refunded', cross-checked against on-chain Refunded events

refund_latency_p50_seconds._description: Median time from order creation to refund completion.
refund_latency_p50_seconds.note: ~12 hours — expected for standard timelock expiry path

refund_latency_p95_seconds._description: 95th-percentile time from order creation to refund completion. Values above timelock + 1800 s signal a coordination problem.

coordinator_uptime_pct_30d._description: Percentage of minutes in the last 30 days the coordinator /healthz endpoint returned HTTP 200.
coordinator_uptime_pct_30d.source: external uptime monitor polling /healthz every 60 s; secondary: Prometheus up{job='oversync-coordinator'}

active_resolver_count._description: Number of resolvers currently staked and active in the ResolverRegistry.
active_resolver_count.source: ResolverRegistry.activeCount() on Sepolia 0x7D9ce70Aa40E144E8BbE266a0dc3b3F91B6D1D99 and oversync-resolver-registry on Stellar testnet

order_lifecycle_duration_avg_seconds._description: Mean time from order creation to completion (claimed on both legs) for completed orders.

failed_orders_by_category._description: Breakdown of failed/refunded orders by failure reason in the measurement window.
failed_orders_by_category.source: coordinator orders WHERE status IN ('failed','refunded'), grouped by failure_reason tag

resolver_no_lock._description: Resolver did not lock destination-side funds within the lock-wait window.

user_no_claim._description: User did not reveal the preimage before the destination-side timelock expired.

rpc_timeout._description: RPC call to Ethereum or Stellar node failed or timed out.

resolver_underfunded._description: Resolver's on-chain balance was insufficient to cover the destination amount.

hashlock_mismatch._description: Destination-side lock hashlock did not match source-side hashlock.

user_cancelled._description: User explicitly cancelled via the frontend refund dialog.

watchdog_refund._description: Background watchdog triggered automatic refund (XLM→ETH swap pending > 5 min).

contract_events_by_chain._description: On-chain event counts for the measurement window, by chain and event type.

ethereum.source: eth_getLogs on HTLCEscrow from block 7820000 to 7834500

OrderCreated._description: New HTLC lock created by a user.

OrderClaimed._description: Resolver revealed preimage and claimed ETH.

OrderRefunded._description: Funds returned to user after timelock expiry.

stellar.source: Soroban RPC getEvents on oversync-htlc, ledger range 2410000–2425000

lock_order._description: HTLC lock created by a resolver on the Stellar side.

claim_order._description: User claimed XLM by revealing the preimage.

refund_order._description: XLM returned to resolver after Stellar-side timelock.

privacy_attestation.note: All values are aggregated counts, durations, or percentages. No per-order identifiers, wallet addresses beyond on-chain public data, or preimage values are present in this snapshot.
